answer:
We can decide I by simulating M for n steps; if it halts earlier, we know it didn’t last n steps.  
Problem III is also decidable because we can simulate and track visited squares, and since the tape is initially blank, the machine can only visit finitely many squares in n steps.  
Problem II is undecidable because after n steps we cannot know if it will ever print a 1 later (this is essentially a version of the halting problem).  

\boxed{J}
